title: Visualization and Video Output Flow
interaction_flow: --- Lyra-1 main inference (sample.py) ---
Caller → visu.py: create_depth_visu(model_output['depths_pred'], cmap='jet', min_max_perc=[0.01,0.99])
visu.py: percentile-normalize depth tensor [B,T,1,H,W], apply cv2.applyColorMap per frame → returns [B,T,3,H,W]
Caller → visu.py: generate_wave_video(image_tensor[B,T_vt,3,H,W], depth_tensor[B,T_vt,1,H,W], wave_length, wave_color*, use_gradient_color)
visu.py: extract image[H,W,3] and depth[H,W] for batch_idx/frame_idx
visu.py → wave_func(depth, curr_depth, wave_length): cosine-squared falloff mask → wave weights
visu.py: prepend pre_frames static frames, animate n_frames+1 wave steps → stack as np array
visu.py: convert to torch.Tensor [1, T+pre_frames, 3, H, W] in [0,1]
Caller → visu.py: save_video(tensor[B,T,C,H,W], outdir, name, fps=8)
visu.py → reshape_video_grid(tensor): einops rearrange batch into grid → [T,C,N1*H,N2*W]
visu.py: to numpy, transpose to [T,H,W,C], scale *255 → uint8
visu.py → imageio.mimwrite(outdir/name.mp4, frames, fps=fps)
--- Wave video calls from sample.py ---
Caller: pred_images_wave = generate_wave_video(...) → save_video(pred_images_wave, outdir_raw, 'rgb_wave_{idx}', fps)
Caller: pred_images_rgb = cat(wave, pred_images) → save_video(pred_images_rgb, outdir_3dgs, 'rgb_{idx}', fps)
Caller: for each view → save_video(pred_images_view, outdir_raw, 'rgb_{idx}_view_idx_{v}', fps)
Caller: save_video(pred_images_out, outdir_full, 'sample_{idx}', fps)  [concat of rgb+depth grid]
Caller: if save_grid → save_video(batch_cat_tensor, outdir_grid, 'sample_grid_{step}', fps)
--- Teaser assembly (dynamic_scene_video_static_scene.py) ---
Caller → create_teaser_video(mp4_paths_or_dir, wave_output_dir, single_output_dir, grid_output_dir, grid_rows, grid_cols, fps)
create_teaser_video: list & natural_sort RGB mp4s from directory (exclude wave/view/grid files)
create_teaser_video → find_subvideos(main_path): parse sample_idx+param, locate 'rgb_wave_{idx}_{param}.mp4' and view mp4s
create_teaser_video → ffmpeg (subprocess): concat wave [+optional mirror/reverse] → wave_output_dir/{sample_idx}.mp4
create_teaser_video → ffmpeg (subprocess): concat views [+optional mirror] → single_output_dir/{sample_idx}.mp4
create_teaser_video → ffmpeg (subprocess): hstack cols + vstack rows of batched single videos → grid_output_dir/grid_{i}.mp4
--- Lyra-2 save path (imaginaire/visualize/video.py) ---
Caller → save_img_or_video(tensor[C,T,H,W], save_fp_wo_ext, fps=24, quality, ffmpeg_params)
save_img_or_video: clamp float or normalize uint8 → [0,1]
if T==1 → PILImage.fromarray → easy_io.dump(save_fp+'.jpg', format='JPEG', quality=85)
else     → rearrange [C,T,H,W]→[T,H,W,C] *255 uint8 → easy_io.dump(save_fp+'.mp4', format='mp4', fps=fps)
Caller → save_video(grid_numpy, video_name, fps=30)
save_video: scale *255, transpose [C,T,H,W]→[T,H,W,C]
save_video → ffmpegcv.VideoWriter(video_name, 'h264', fps): write frame-by-frame (cv2.cvtColor RGB→BGR)
key_relationships: sample.py imports from src.utils.visu: create_depth_visu, generate_wave_video, save_video
generate_wave_video internally calls wave_func (cosine-squared depth falloff)
save_img_or_video (imaginaire/video.py) delegates to easy_io.dump for both image and video
cosmos_predict1/visualize/video.py mirrors imaginaire/visualize/video.py (same logic, different imports)

function_signatures.save_video_visu: save_video(test_video_out: Tensor[B,T,C,H,W], outdir: str, name='sample_grid', fps=8) -> None
function_signatures.wave_func: wave_func(values: ndarray, wave_pos: float, wave_length=1.0) -> ndarray  # cosine-sq falloff
function_signatures.generate_wave_video: generate_wave_video(image_tensor[B,T,3,H,W], depth_tensor[B,T,1,H,W], batch_idx, frame_idx, n_frames=24, wave_length=1.0, wave_color, wave_color_front, wave_color_back, use_gradient_color, pre_frames=24) -> Tensor[1,T+pre,3,H,W]
function_signatures.create_depth_visu: create_depth_visu(x: Tensor[B,T,C,H,W], cmap='jet', data_range=None, out_float=True, min_max_perc=[0.01,0.99]) -> Tensor[B,T,3,H,W]
function_signatures.create_teaser_video: create_teaser_video(mp4_paths_or_dir: Union[List[str],str], wave_output_dir, single_output_dir, grid_output_dir=None, grid_rows=2, grid_cols=3, use_mirror_views=False, use_mirror_wave=False, fps=30) -> None
function_signatures.save_img_or_video: save_img_or_video(sample_C_T_H_W_in01: Tensor, save_fp_wo_ext: Union[str,IO], fps=24, quality=None, ffmpeg_params=None) -> None
function_signatures.save_video_imaginaire: save_video(grid: ndarray, video_name: str, fps=30) -> None

data_formats.create_depth_visu_output: Tensor [B,T,3,H,W] colorized, float in [0,1] or uint8

output_formats.create_teaser_video_wave: MP4 via ffmpeg subprocess, libx264 crf=18 preset=fast
output_formats.create_teaser_video_single: MP4 via ffmpeg subprocess, libx264 crf=18 preset=fast
output_formats.create_teaser_video_grid: MP4 via ffmpeg hstack/vstack filter_complex, libx264

important_notes: Two parallel save_video implementations exist: (1) Lyra-1 src/utils/visu.py uses imageio.mimwrite; (2) Lyra-1 cosmos_predict1 and Lyra-2 imaginaire both use ffmpegcv.VideoWriter. create_teaser_video is a post-processing CLI tool that reads previously-saved MP4s and re-encodes via ffmpeg subprocess for teaser assembly. Wave video uses depth-guided front-to-back cosine wave sweep with optional gradient coloring. Grid layout uses N1xN2 einops rearrange (perfect-square batch or 1xB).
